mission.title: Install and Configure MetalLB (Bare-metal Load Balancer)
mission.description: MetalLB is a load-balancer implementation for bare metal Kubernetes clusters, utilizing standard routing protocols. It is ideal for environments where cloud load balancers are not available, providing a way to expose services externally. Key trade-offs include the need for proper network configuration and potential complexity in setup compared to cloud-native solutions.

steps.title: Add the MetalLB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official MetalLB Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add metallb https://metallb.universe.tf

```

> This command registers the MetalLB repository.

steps.title: Install MetalLB Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for MetalLB to function correctly, defining the custom resources it will manage.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metallb/metallb/v0.15.3/manifests/namespace.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metallb/metallb/v0.15.3/manifests/metallb.yaml

```

> Creates the MetalLB namespace.
> Deploys the MetalLB components.

steps.title: Install MetalLB using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs MetalLB into the Kubernetes cluster using the Helm package manager.

```bash

helm install metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --create-namespace --version 0.15.3

```

> Installs MetalLB in the metallb-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a MetalLB ConfigMap
steps.description:
This ConfigMap defines the IP address pool that MetalLB will use to allocate external IPs for services.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    address-pools:
    - name: default
      protocol: layer2
      addresses:
      - 192.168.1.200-192.168.1.250
EOF

```

> Adjust the IP range according to your network.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    address-pools:
    - name: default
      protocol: layer2
      addresses:
      - 192.168.1.200-192.168.1.250
EOF

steps.title: Verify MetalLB installation
steps.description:
This step checks that MetalLB is running correctly and ready to allocate IPs.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n metallb-system

kubectl get svc -n metallb-system

```

> Ensure all MetalLB pods are in 'Running' state.
> Check that MetalLB services are correctly set up.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MetalLB
uninstall.description:
This step removes MetalLB from the cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss if services are relying on MetalLB for external access.

```bash

helm uninstall metallb --namespace metallb-system

kubectl delete namespace metallb-system

```

> Uninstalls the MetalLB Helm release.
> Removes the MetalLB namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MetalLB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configuration. This step includes draining any workloads if necessary, upgrading MetalLB, and verifying the installation post-upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get configmap config -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml

helm upgrade metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --version 0.15.3

kubectl get pods -n metallb-system

```

> Backup the current MetalLB configuration.
> Upgrades MetalLB to the specified version.
> Verify that all MetalLB pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get configmap config -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to allocate IP address'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the IP address pool is exhausted or incorrectly configured.

**Fix:**
Check the ConfigMap for the correct IP address range and ensure there are available IPs.

troubleshooting.title: MetalLB pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen due to misconfiguration or missing permissions.

**Fix:**
Review the logs of the MetalLB pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n metallb-system' and correct any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Services are not getting external IPs
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that MetalLB is not properly configured or the service annotations are missing.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service has the correct annotations for MetalLB and that the ConfigMap is correctly set up.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No available IP addresses'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the configured address pool is fully allocated.

**Fix:**
Expand the address pool in the MetalLB ConfigMap or free up some IPs by deleting services.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for BGP peering, improved layer2 mode stability, and enhanced logging capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.14, review the new BGP configuration options in the documentation.

resolution.summary: When everything is working, you should see MetalLB pods in the 'Running' state and services with external IPs allocated from the specified address pool.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster is set up and accessible, with the necessary tools installed.
